reviews.review_description: Random download with Horizon+. Definitely one I'll be buying to own. It's so simple but yet challenging. I wouldn't change a thing. But if I could. I'd add multiplayer. It would be a blast! Definitely worth the cost on its own. Definitely  worth the download while it's on Horizon+

This game is a hidden jim i highly recommend it for anybody into space exploration and rogue likes i'm not a big fan of the normal stellaris games because i don't wanna learn what a billion buttons do but this game is so simple and fun and relaxing because it doesn't require very much physical movement this game is a must buy

I love FTL and Star Trek Voyager…this game is the perfect mix of them…I love the replay ability of the game. The game’s music and atmosphere mixed with QuestGameOptimizer to punch up the graphics makes this a must buy for any rouge like space game enthusiasts…11/10
This is game is so good, balanced, and well thought out. I can finish a run in a little over 1h which is perfect.

This game allows you to control a small ship in space using layers and rockets to defend yourself. The hand tracking makes it fun and easy to learn, and each run through is different giving it high replay-ability. The part that really stands out to me is the atmosphere. You are literally in outerspace seeing planets, spacestations, blackholes, eat. You can see lightning storms moving across the surface, or city lights on the planets night side. It is the small attention to detail that makes this game a hidden gem!
Hidden gem, really! Lots of fun!

I really enjoy the game, it reminds me a lot of from other suns minus the fps aspect. Great looking little pockets of space to fight in and 2$ is an insanely good deal if you get it on sale for that.  

The controls are killing me and take some getting used to. Going faster is an All or Nothing button push on a timer, and to move you plot courses by holding down a button while moving a line then hold down trigger button as well to make a secondary line. Would be so much easier to just give standard drone type controls and so much more fun flying around these areas and asteroid fields. 

Targeting is tricky to figure out but works pretty well when you get the hang of it. Even then though it can be really hard to Target that missile about to hit you or that aircraft carrier spamming Fighters at you when there's a lot of targets in the area.

Would love controls that mirror other flight or Chopper games instead, with a set amount of booster than can be used selectively here and there.

Make a button that can cycle through targets and if you hold it down it locks on to that Target. 

Also, a perpetual exploration type mode would be great.

This is a pretty good game. But let me lay some facts. Yes it’s true if you die you restart but not completely. If you die VI the AI robot helper aboard your ship tells you that you can research upgrades for your ship.  Thats only in the main menu if you die. Once you purchase them via research points the upgrades stay with you forever making it easier to beat enemy’s and advance.  So if you die that’s okay    Just don’t forget to use the research points you collected to upgrade your ship.

I have this game also on steam, if it is on sale it is a bargain on all plattforms. Sadly the developer "forgott" to optimize it for quest 3 and does not use the capabilities of xr 2 gen 2, so you have to do it for yourself e.g. otherwise it is blurry ( minus one star for that ). Quest games optimizer can help. Rendertarget 2500x2500 @ 72 fps is no problem.

The game seems VERY complicated at first. A robust AI as a helper, absolutely stunning backdrops (think Homeworld or Sins of a Solar Empire) and fun battles.

But then it...plateaus. Quickly. 

The battles...are always the same. The randomly generated maps...are always the same (you'll eventually get used to them). The random encounters? Sometimes fun. Exploration per board? Nil.

But I don't know if I'm expecting too much...like a storyline. Or plot. Both of which seem to be missing. 

If you go in expecting a steep learning curve and then a quick plateau as far as novelty, set in a beautiful backdrop, you'll be fine. Want anything else, and you'll go back to the store, looking for something else.

The game is well done, graphics, sound, voice acting but after reading the reviews about the controls I see what they are saying but in context. 

The game is a pc game that uses a mouse to move  which is natural movement. Adapting mouse movements with clicks do not translate in vr well unless the controls are re-imaged. 

Guess lost in translation applies…..
only wish i could move the menues upwards to play while laying down

The game immerses you in a mysterious atmosphere. With every death, the mystery of why you come back to life unfolds, each time bringing new experience and skills. Eventually, you just want to sit for a while, listen to the background music, gaze at the stars, and ponder existential questions

This is a VR version of Crying Suns.  Loads and loads to unlock via a currency you keep even if you die. No microtransactions, controls at first feel a bit difficult but you soon pick them up once you do multiple runs. Very comfortable to play. Finally decently priced. Recommended
So i cant even start the game idk why

I. Do like how the game looks etc but my issue is the Controls, movement is soo hard to get used to, why can't we just fly around with thr thumb sticks? Pressing a button to go to a point whilst trying to defend your ship you are almost certain to die.
The controls for this game make no sense it doesn't let me shoot anything all I can do is move around can't lock targets or anything it's pointless and it's annoying.
In the start i found the controls weird but now im used to it, the music is awesome, and every level is a cool new space thing in the background, like a huge alien or planet.
I got a the game and it was super cool

I really like the game, it certainly has a challenge to it!
But all UI elements are so close to you that just by having your controllers on your lap you press buttons, forcing you to move back every time you open a menu.

Really playable fun game.
I like that a play through takes under an hour 
I like all the in game upgrades 
I like the long term upgrades
I like the different ships offering different play styles 
I like the challenge modes.
Its a good game but i would like to see a multiplayer mode for co-op and pvp

But it’s simple. Limiting. A great deal of upgrades for your progression is left up to chance. And the combination of chance based upgrades with purchased permanent upgrades really confuses what to focus on.

This game is certainly polished and I think many will enjoy it. If you want complexity, this might not be for you. It’s more like a mobile game for quick hits.

reviews.review_title: Fun not my cup of tea
reviews.review_description: That’s about it
Just could not stop playing this game. only paid £2 on discount. Feel like I ripped off the company.
If you die ALL of your progress restarts it gets really boring to

I’m having a lot of fun with this game.
Interesting story 
Quick and easy to jump in and out 
Definitely worth buying
The immersion is fantastic, only 20 minutes in and I'm in love.well done to the devs on this beauty

reviews.review_title: This game perfects what consoles have...
reviews.review_description: This game perfects what co soles and PC games have failed at. The ability to have interstellar battles and put your point of view in ANY dimension!!! Plus, this game allows you to pause the game and adjust your point of view. This game is highly polished and the music is epic, the controls and everything is very smooth. One couldn't ask for a better game for only 10 dollars!

Not to long you can finish in less than 2h but with good replayability so fun to finish at least several times for story to progress. Spent on this title multiple hours. Not only for Stellaris fans as althought in same universe the game has little to do with the Stellaris title.

reviews.review_title: Must buy for sci-fi strategy arcade fans
reviews.review_description:
The game offers an unbelievable fantasticly satisfying learning curve.  I found with each mission i was becoming a more efficient pilot, with increased accuracy, increased decision making, and better strategy.  

You really have to learn all the enemies and know their weapons and piloting ability in order to progress to the highest levels... as well there are three ships you must master too.

I like the games implimentation of simple weapons selections(blasters, lasers, and missiles) and how you obtain loadout variety through acquisition of augmentations to those three weapons.  Knowing which upgrades pair best with which ships is key to making best use of each of the three hero ships.

For $10 as a purchase... as bubbles from Trailer Park Boys said in the movie Countdown to liquor day... "i believe i have achieved satisfaction".

An exceptionally well made and polished roguelite that's easy to pick up but hard to master. It's a relaxing experience that somehow never loses it's edge. Stunningly vivid sci fi backdrops, atmospheric audio design and intuitive strategic gameplay put you in control of dynamic space battles. 

Incredible how this game manages to remain engaging despite its simplicity. Should appeal to anyone who loves tactical combat, roguelites or just science fiction in general.
My title takes care of my review. Thank you for making a cool game.
Or else
So much fun!
Really fun to just sit down, relax, and play the game.
Beautiful and simple rogue light where you captain a spaceship through the universe. It’s simple but hard and the game that I often turn to when I wanna play something but am not sure what.

The ships move in 3D space, which is good. Graphics and sound are not bad. However, the controls and UI are very unintuitive for me. I struggle to remember which buttons to press, even after 40 minutes of gameplay. Therefore it's too stressful and frustrating to be enjoyable. In comparison, I enjoy FTL very much.

Great game could spend plenty of time on it…But got a problem…there is no “auto save”…and when I finish a sector and it tells me to start on the new sector…I can’t find a way to save what I just finished…can use some help..so I can keep on playing this great game!!!

This game would be a good (but not great compared to other offerings) single player space roguelite if it was not in VR.  The problem is VR adds nothing to this experience, and overall makes it worse due to poorer controls and graphics.  Again, it isn't a bad game, it isn't even a bad VR game, but there was absolutely no reason for it to be in VR.
I love how when you die you don’t retain your perk thingys but you can research stuff to better your ship and voyages. Plus Vir is super cool.

The gameplay is good.  It's a slow progression roguelite space "rts" kindof.  It's a bit too much of a mobile game, it lacks depth or significant meaning or progression.  You can beat it in like 10 sittings or so.  After that it's more a collection game and going back to see what little storyline pieces or mystery you missed.  It's good while it lasts, but consider it like a mobile game you'll play for a couple weeks and then may or may not come back to.  The 3 stars just reflects lack of real meat beyond the initial experience.

The game is very short and progress does not carry over between deaths. You upgrade your ship, die, lose everything and upgrade your ship again with xp gained from that round.. After beating the game you are supposed to do like 7 runs to get the true ending. Levels never change, and once you figure patterns out it becomes a loading screen burdened chore. I enjoyed it, but became annoyed fast after beating it once (takes like an hour).
Would not recommend.

One of my favorite quest games. Cool environments, unique gameplay and combat, and enough content to make the game worth replaying. 

It is a rogue-lite which means expect to die and start over a lot, but you can spend your xp between runs to upgrade your ship. 

If you like roguelikes or space combat, this game is for you.
The gameplay in this is very satisfying and the visuals and whole vibe is just right.

Set in the stellaris game franchise, this casual tactical space shooter present a very good story. With a rich background that begs to be discovered and beautiful settings in which you play this game will give you a lot of enjoyment. However some of his short coming slowly turns this into a luck base game toward the end. I really love this game and I can't wait for it's sequel.

reviews.review_title: Games was very interesting in the beginn
reviews.review_description:
Game is very interesting, but after you finish it 10 times, it becomes boring to go via same levels again and again. 
Would be a 5 if there would be much more lvl, but in any case it's worth spending in game several hours

I’m normally not a fan of roguelikes. But this works. It’s a great looking sci-fi strategy. You control your space ship, jumping from place to play, mining, fighting and exploring your way around a galaxy to solve a mystery. Every time you die, you restart, but you collect research points that you can spend on permanent upgrades that last even when you die. So you’re always slowly getting more powerful. The combat system is great and works well, it pauses when you plot a course but switching weapons and combat is in real time. The sound effect and graphics are fantastic. A lot of work has gone into this! It’s like watching the battle scenes from a sci-fi movie or TV show. The background/skyboxes are next level. I consider this a must buy, it works so well in 3D/VR. I hope to see more from this dev (a full 4X would be amazing!).

The game and its looks are great.The movement is kinda like a game that to be able to move you have to boost but the worst part of the game is when your about to finish a part of the game you get reset.The reset is extremely annoying and always makes me angry.What also makes me angry is upgrades on your adventure.I wish i could get two upgrades but you can only get one.If you like games that make you feel like your about to lose everything buy this.Don't get this game if you want a laidback experience.Still even if you want it get it you should get it.This is just my opinion so you might wanna take it with a grain of salt.

If you are someone who likes to repeat things...this is the game for you.  Repeat, Repeat, Repeat.  The game actually has very little to do with skill and how far you make it each time before being sent to the start to REPEAT is more based on luck of the draw.  I spent a great deal of time repeating this until I finally stopped using the game completely.  Waste of time in my book.

Edit: Ok, Ive sunk some hours into this game and im editing my review.
While it is fun, for a short while, It really starts to get VERY repetitive. Yeah, the game is a time-loop, but using that as an excuse for boring/repetitive gameplay is just bad.

The game feels unfinished, empty and unfulfilling after just a short while (2 hours) And finishing the whole thing takes less than an hour, with you just buying upgrades and unlocking logs after every go-through.

This game is overpriced at 20 bucks. 10 would fit it much better, until/If it actually gets some... you know... Content.
Ive played free games on my phone with way more content, and 5 bucks games on my pc with MANY times more content than those phone games...

A couple hours played right now and this is one of the best experiences I have had on the quest so far. Many of the games were starting to feel a bit stale and a repeat of gameplay from previous releases, this brings something new to the quest and I hope we start to see more creativity in game design like this. It's very similar to FTL-like games but with an increased focus on combat and a faster pace. The game has a great atmosphere, really nice controls and meaningful decisions to be made along the way which will impact your outcome of each run. Going forward I hope the dev team continues to support the game by adding in new events, ships, bosses etc to further increase the diversity of each play-through.

I enjoyed this game immensely. The leveling up progression is well balanced and the graphics are spectacular. The controls took a bit of getting used to but work well. This game is worth getting. 

Having beaten the game, I hope the developers continue to develop the game. Some minor balancing is needed (defensive mods are not useful). More depth in progression would be wonderful, I want to keep upgrading my ship! The ending felt unsatisfactory and more could be done for replayability. Multiplayer would be great. Battles can get a bit stale and incorporating new elements besides asteroids in a large rectangle would be a good step.

This game is polished, bug-free, enjoyable and had potential to be even more. I look forward to updates and more content!
Not sure how many times I would replay this, but it was definitely worth whatever I paid. Fun, good strategy, nice graphics. Totally worth the purchase is strategy is your jam.

reviews.review_title: Incredible, one of if not the best
reviews.review_description:
Having owned my Quest for over a year I have really struggled to be motivated to use it. As soon as I heard about a Stellaris game I really hoped it would be the answer and all I can say is wow, it really is.

The detail, the variety, the gameplay it’s all there in spades. Exploration, loot, battles that really make you think instead of mindless shooting. It really is a game changer for me and my Quest

The back drops of each system are amazing, you can just sit looking around and see so much detail everywhere.

Well done to the devs, you’ve created a fantastic game and even better a unique brilliant experience. I can’t wait to play some more already!

developer_response.body: Many thanks for your kind words and for taking the time to leave a review!

reviews.review_description:
Exactly as the title says, this is a roguelike built ground-up for VR. The controls and are simple and intuitive, the graphics are great for a quest game and the music fits really nicely. As a long-time Stellaris player, it's cool to see all the references it gets, but Ghost Signal definitely stands as its own game.

I did find one bug, on the mission where you choose to help one faction destroy the other. When allied ships were destroyed they spawned objects that targeted me and did huge damage when they hit, but because they were 'allied', I couldn't destroy them.

Ghost Signal reminds me a lot of FTL, and with that thought I'd like to see some more complexity to long-term gameplay. Something similar to crew members gaining experience over time, or maybe another strategic resource to manage. I also think components could be tweaked a bit. None of them feel truly distinct, like FTL's components do, because they mostly just provide small bonuses and encourage an existing playstyle instead of introducing a new one. Maybe introduce a new tier of more powerful components but limit them to 1 at a time?

A 'follow ship' option so I don't have to chase after the Aurora would also be great :)

Edit: A better way to activate time slow would be great, since telling the Aurora to fire automatically cancels movement. You could also add time-related components that increase its duration or effect.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thank you so much for taking the time to review the game and share your feedback + bug report with us! We've noted the bug and will look into it. :)

reviews.review_title: Very fun game but it has issues
reviews.review_description:
First of all let just get this out of the way: this game is very good and a fun space VR experience with well thought of combat. But...
The game is unbalanced:
1) The components: Despite the wide variety, most of them are useless. This is a game where offence is literally the best defence. This brings most runs (especially later when traders sell only a single item) into a feast or famine situation where you either get the offensive damage modules (NOT for missiles, I explain at [3]) and melt through enemies before they can touch you or you get random defensive or speed garbage and you fall into a snoozefest of waiting for ages for your weapons to reload again and again before you can kill big stuff, while they barrage you with avoidable AND unavoidable attacks (no amount of defensive modules outweigh the strength of offensive ones).
2) The routing: There is a signle effective strategy, get the most elite combat systems, period. It is the only way to get possibly good components. The creature systems are a very cool VR experience but balance-wise they offer worthless components (except the ether drake). The ??? on the other hand have a wide range of outcomes both negative (gain defect/lose hull/lose scrap/nothing happens) and positive (gain/upgrade random components/gain scrap/repair), but it is highly skewed to the negatives (nothing happens IS negative since you want to fight to get stuff).
3) The defects: There is a small pool of them and, just like the components, some of them are destructive and some are inconsequential. In fact, there is just 2 defects that actually matter: -25% dmg dealt and no missiles. The rest are mostly irrelevant.
(Un)fun fact: The final boss gives you random defects. This makes missile strategies statistically useless since you might not be able to actually fire missiles. On the other hand, the boss might give you defects like "some systems are hidden in the map" or "you take damage when you get new components" (emphasis on FINAL boss)
It is a bit like FTL.

TrampStyle said exactly what I was thinking. Read his review and you’ll know exactly what I think of the game. Really hoping there’s an update to clear some of these problems up. It gets really old starting from scratch every time with the slow progression system. This game is SOOOO close, it could easily become 5 stars with just a few tweaks

It was surprise for me. This game is really good. I was sceptical at first glance, but after a while it clicked. Gameplay is simple.
I have few suggestions for improvement:
- when collecting money, research and others popup saying how much was collected hangs in front of my face and blocks view, please move it a little bit down,
- when on loading screen enable right hand, so I can check how many missiles i have, it will make loading screens less dull,
- please add option to adjust how far away menu is, as an adult i need to keep my hands close to body so i don't click something accidentally

reviews.review_title: This game will get me in trouble.
reviews.review_description:
Very busy and hardly get any quest time, but liked the look of this one, and was recomended on a FB group I am in. Down loaded and making time to play. 😊
Took a while to master the controls (not played anything for a while, so probably just me.) Game looks fantastic, very playable so far, hoping it will get more intense as the game progresses. 🤞
Good value I would say and would recommend to have it in your library.

Typically I'm used to fps action on roguelikes, but this is an interesting spin on it, and a welcome one! you're patrolling a space ship from a third person view, kinda like action and strategy in one, with traditional roguelike aspects.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review, we're so happy you liked it!

reviews.review_description:
What an amazing way to bring the Stellaris franchise into new light. The game is peaceful, yet action packed. Floating through space is a beautiful background, and the movement of the ship is a beautifully unique mechanic. 

The game is also story rich, which can't be said for all roguelites. After my first run ended, I loved the explanation for why the gameplay loop makes sense in-universe. 

The controls were easy to understand, and within seconds of the tutorial I understood how to play. Despite the ease of learning, the game never became boring, as new secrets and features were unlocked long the way.

I did experience some trouble with the hand tracking, but it wasn't immense, and I could play just fine with the controllers.

Overall, this game is relaxing yet engaging, and is the perfect addition to any VR library.

reviews.review_title: Tried the trial, Bought it the moment it
reviews.review_description:
Tutorial could use some work (it might just be me being stupid but i do not remember a tut section on grip movement), and the button touch option sometimes registers the other fingers pressing the other options, but so far i absolutely adore this game.
Especially when i saw the tyanki in the background of the creature sector.
Really looking forward to exploring more. And i am hoping for, perhaps different skins in the future?
Waiting for my headset to recharge so I can eagerly hop back in.
Use my referral link to buy Ghost Signal: A Stellaris Game and you can get 25% off your purchase www.oculus .com/app referrals/JenRodg/4302631119827554/?utm_source=3

The game looks nice and plays nice in my opinion.
The movement seems a little off but works fine after some minutes.

The only complain is that the game is just too easy(casual) for my taste
Loads of fun with great replayability. It’s slay the spire in space VR.
The controls are exceptional and fun to use, great progression, a massive amount of ship upgrades and unlockables during the game. I highly recommended this game.

I’ve been playing this a lot and enjoying it. I was disappointed to learn that, after finishing the first world, you get a new ship and do it again. I’ll give this game 5 ⭐️s if they add more content.

If you played Eternal starlight and found it fun, then you probably wil like this.
In essence the game is you and your little ship goes from sector to sector doing multiple space based tasks (it involves mostly weapons).
You  control you ship by laying down a waypoint line of a fixed distance, the space where 1 can fly freely  is a big enough arena, you can shoot, scan(collect minerals) and fly.
Not much but the key is its simplicity and its diversity.
Every game has traders and different goodies, every time you play (and die)collect resources that make your ship a bit better the next time you play.
Story wise, wel the story is, there is a cosmic explosion, all communication gets broken, you need to find out whats happened, so fly from sector to sector. You die? You restart like you went back in time. And except for the research points collected, that you can invest, you have to restart fresh in a new randomized map

Immersive and exhilarating, this VR game seamlessly blends stunning visuals with responsive controls!Unforgettable journey into the virtual realm for sure. Use my referral below for a friend discount! (Make the , a . And take the parenthesis out). A+ game

Bio,link(/)metalkitten

Hours and hours of fun !!!  
https: // bio • link / callmecoco Get Twentyfive Percent Off!!!

...but I really love space exploration games, and Stellaris has by its side a good mix of combat (with a good balance of speed and strategy), exploration and trading, with a really thorough and intuitive navigation and combat interface that makes the play really enjoyable. Worth buying.

reviews.review_title: Top notch sci-fi rouge like gameplay
reviews.review_description: This game is highly recommended for fans of space shooters, strategy games, rouge likes, and even RPG elements thrown in to unlock and upgrade. I really dig the random occurrences to explore planets for upgrades and items, meeting new alien races and bartering, encounters with massive cosmic monsters, and even planet shattering boss battles. I can’t say enough good stuff about this game, I’ve been playing for almost 100 hours and haven’t experienced a single bug, glitch or crash. Well done development team 💪🏼 DM me and I will happily share my code to save you 20% off if this game sounds like something you’d like to try! You won’t regret it!
Really cool game!

Rogue-lite game that has similarities to FTL, if you ever played that. It does not, however, have crew/ship dynamics like FTL. Instead, you fight ship to ship battles as you travel to sectors (the sectors behave like FTL. You can choose your path.). 

Zero motion sickness in this, as you have an outside view of the sector/ship. 

After you die on a run, you can spend certain gathered “money” to research upgrades that will stick with you through death, so you’ll get stronger after each run. 

Overall, very cool and enjoyable game.

I’m simply amazed and addicted to this game. The gameplay is amazing, I always feel an urge to do another run. Also, the randomness keeps it addicting. The small details and easy to learn hard to play controls makes it a must have. Definitely recommend this!

I think this game is very well designed and executed. The devs are doing an amazing job taking advantage of every bit of cpu power in the quest 2. I would give 5* if it wasnt for the game play its self that seems to be a test in endurance and humility. After killing about 125 baddies, with superior weapons and ever increasing armor and ship stats, I still get killed like for the 22nd time in 8 hours straight. I think this is how far my masochism stretches and I will go fishing or something. I really wish they would use all this know-how and prowess to make a game like Sid’s Pirates! Or maybe something like Sid’s Starships ? I think this game’s genre is not something most players would enjoy much.
I am spechless i just beat the game it does get stressful but its worth it i cant stop playing.
Great game with hours of enjoyment would benefit from a quest 3 graphics to upgrade but still amazing
